rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STANLEY CHONG KWONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. 1) The sole purpose of the hearing is to determine why there has been no response to the Examiner notes: a. Since 04/18/2022 the tentative ruling has indicated the 2-year account must begin with Decedent's date of death. Decedent died 07/08/2009. This account begins 10/01/2019. A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. [LRSF 14.34]. Unless otherwise permitted for good cause, if an account exceeds two years, the first two years must be filed separately. After the first two-year account is adjudicated, subsequent account periods may not be for more than three (3) years and each three-year period must be filed separately. [LRSF 14.34 A.] b. All pleadings filed in proceedings under the Probate Code must be verified. If two or more persons join in a pleading, it may be verified by any of them. [CRC 7.103]. c. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.34 D.] d. On Schedule D disbursements Executor must explain and provide the date of the court order authorizing legal fees for each entry and the case number. e. Executor must give 15 days' notice of the petition.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DELIA W. FOSTER 1995 TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Respondent Gary E. Botto To Further Respond To Second Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Delia Foster established the Delia W. Foster 1995 Trust on June 19, 1995, naming herself as trustee. The Trust was allegedly drafted by Respondent Gary Botto and/or Respondent law firm Botto Law Group. Botto was named successor trustee. Foster executed the Twelfth and final amendment to the Trust on March 30, 2015. The amendment was allegedly drafted by Respondent Botto and/or his law firm, and Botto was still named successor trustee. Foster allegedly named Botto her attorney in fact in 2015. Foster died on May 21, 2019. Petitioners are Foster's nephews and heirs at law. On August 27, 2020, they filed Petition for (1) Order Compelling Trust Accounting; (2) Order Compelling Accounting by Attorney in Fact; (3) Breach of Fiduciary Duty; (4) Professional Negligence; (5) Return of Trust Property (Prob. Code section 850); (6) Financial Elder Abuse; (7) Removal of Trustee; (8) Immediate Suspension of Trustee's Powers Pending Removal; and (9) Order Instruction Trustee to Refrain from Using Trust Assets to Defend, naming Botto and Botto Law Group among the Respondents. On February 1, 2022, Petitioners filed Motion to Compel Respondent Gary E. Botto to Further Respond to Second Request for Production of Documents, and Motion to Compel Respondent Botto Law Group to Further Respond to Second Request for Production of Documents. Petitioners seek further production of redacted billing records in response to three document requests, under CCP sections 2031.310 and 2031.320. After meet and confer efforts by the parties, Petitioners now seek redacted billing records for all of Botto Law Group's clients, for the years 2019 and 2020, showing (1) the date of entry, (2) the attorney's name, and (3) the amount of time billed for each entry. This, Petitioner's argue, will allow them to see how many hours each attorney was billing on daily basis. On February 10, 2022, Botto and Botto Law Group filed Motion for Protective Order, Request for Sanctions. That motion will be heard concurrently with the Petitioners' two motions to compel further responses to request for production of documents. The Motion to Compel Respondent Gary E. Botto to Further Respond to Second Request for Production of Documents is GRANTED. Document production is limited to all 2019 and 2020 billing records, showing (1) the date of entry, (2) the attorney's name, and (3) the amount of time billed for each entry. All client specific information and description of specific work done otherwise subject to privilege, privacy, or attorney work product protections may be redacted or omitted. To be "discoverable," information must be "relevant to the subject matter" and reasonably calculated to lead to discovery of admissible evidence (CCP section 2017.010). Information is "relevant to the subject matter" if it might "reasonably assist a party in evaluating the case, preparing for trial, or facilitating settlement thereof." (City of Los Angeles v. Sup. Ct. (2017) 9 Cal.App.5th 272, 288.) Petitioners allege that Botto and Botto Law Group overbilled Foster and the Foster Trust intentionally and knowingly. The requested information might reasonably assist Petitioners in evaluating their allegations and - depending on what the information shows - may help facilitate settlement. While the information about other client account billing may or may not be admissible, it may lead to discovery of admissible evidence of the alleged breaches of duty and elder abuse. "[E]vidence of other transactions to show motive, intent, knowledge, plan, and absence of mistake" is admissible. (Kirkland v. Sup. Ct. (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 92, 98, quoting Morris Stulsaft Foundation v. Sup. Ct. (1966) 245 Cal.App.2d 409, 422-23.) [Part 1 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DELIA W. FOSTER 1995 TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Delia Foster established the Delia W. Foster 1995 Trust on June 19, 1995, naming herself as trustee. The Trust was allegedly drafted by Respondent Gary Botto and/or Respondent law firm Botto Law Group. Botto was named successor trustee. Foster executed the Twelfth and final amendment to the Trust on March 30, 2015. The amendment was allegedly drafted by Respondent Botto and/or his law firm, and Botto was still named successor trustee. Foster allegedly named Botto her attorney in fact in 2015. Foster died on May 21, 2019. Petitioners are Foster's nephews and heirs at law. On August 27, 2020, they filed Petition for (1) Order Compelling Trust Accounting; (2) Order Compelling Accounting by Attorney in Fact; (3) Breach of Fiduciary Duty; (4) Professional Negligence; (5) Return of Trust Property (Prob. Code section 850); (6) Financial Elder Abuse; (7) Removal of Trustee; (8) Immediate Suspension of Trustee's Powers Pending Removal; and (9) Order Instruction Trustee to Refrain from Using Trust Assets to Defend, naming Botto and Botto Law Group among the Respondents. On February 1, 2022, Petitioners filed Motion to Compel Respondent Gary E. Botto to Further Respond to Second Request for Production of Documents, and Motion to Compel Respondent Botto Law Group to Further Respond to Second Request for Production of Documents. On February 1, 2022, Petitioners filed Motion to Compel Respondent Gary E. Botto to Further Respond to Second Request for Production of Documents, and Motion to Compel Respondent Botto Law Group to Further Respond to Second Request for Production of Documents. These motions to compel are set to be heard concurrently with the instant motion for protective order and sanctions. On February 10, 2022, Petitioners filed Motion to Compel Respondent Gary E. Botto to Provide Further Responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One, and Motion to Compel Respondent Botto Law Group to Provide Further Responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One. On July 19, 2022, after hearing on July 13, 2022, the court granted Petitioners' Motion to Compel Respondent Gary E. Botto to Further Respond to Second Request for Production of Documents, and Motion to Compel Respondent Botto Law Group to Further Respond to Second Request for Production of Documents, and imposed monetary sanctions on Botto and Botto Law Group under the Civil Discovery Act. On February 10, 2022, Respondents Botto and Botto Law Group filed the instant Motion for Protective Order, Request for Sanctions. In it, they seek a protective order from this court under Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.020, 2031.060, and under the court's inherent powers to regulate proceedings, Code of Civil Procedure section 128, in response to Petitioners' requests for production of discovery responses related to Botto Law Group's other client accounts. They also seek monetary sanctions against Petitioners for misuse of discovery, under Code of Civil Procedure sections 2023.010 and 2023.030. Respondents' Botto and Botto Law Group's Motion for Protective Order, Request for Sanctions is DENIED. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.060, (a) When an inspection, copying, testing, or sampling of documents, tangible things, places, or electronically stored information has been demanded, the party to whom the demand has been directed, and any other party or affected person, may promptly move for a protective order. This motion shall be accompanied by a meet and confer declaration under Section 2016.040. (b) The court, for good cause shown, may make any order that justice requires to protect any party or other person from unwarranted annoyance, embarrassment, or oppression, or undue burden and expense. [Part 1 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DELIA W. FOSTER 1995 TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order; Request For Sanctions (Added For The Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 3] As the court has stated in orders granting the recent related motions to compel responses to discovery in this case, to be "discoverable," information must be "relevant to the subject matter" and reasonably calculated to lead to discovery of admissible evidence (CCP section 2017.010). Information is "relevant to the subject matter" if it might "reasonably assist a party in evaluating the case, preparing for trial, or facilitating settlement thereof." (City of Low Angeles v. Sup. Ct. (2017) 9 Cal.App.5th 272, 288.) Petitioners allege that Botto and Botto Law Group overbilled Foster and the Foster Trust intentionally and knowingly. The requested information might reasonably assist Petitioners in evaluating their allegations and - depending on what the information shows - may help facilitate settlement. While the information about other client accounts may or may not be admissible, it may lead to discovery of admissible evidence of the alleged breaches of duty and elder abuse on Foster and the Foster Trust. "[E]vidence of other transactions to show motive, intent, knowledge, plan, and absence of mistake" is admissible. (Kirkland v. Sup. Ct. (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 92, 98, quoting Morris Stulsaft Foundation v. Sup. Ct. (1966) 245 Cal.App.2d 409, 422-23.) Petitioners shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a hard-copy proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling prior to the hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day prior to the hearing. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 3 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DELIA W. FOSTER 1995 TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order; Request For Sanctions (Added For The Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] As an initial issue, the motion fails to state what demand or demands against which it seeks the Court's protection from "unwarranted annoyance, embarrassment, or oppression, or undue burden and expense." The Notice of Motion states on the first page, second paragraph, "This motion is made pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure sections 2031.060 on the grounds that Respondents should not be required to further produce any documents, with sanctions requests under the same code section in addition to section 2023 for abuse of discovery." (Notice of Motion, 1:21-24, emphasis added.) The motion states at the bottom of page six, "Respondents [] now seek the protection of the Court with respect to the production of information and documents regarding other clients who are wholly unrated to Petitioner's claims regarding Decedent Delia Foster." (Motion, 6:21-24). As listed above, Petitioners filed four total motions to compel further responses to propounded discovery against Respondents Botto and Botto Law Group in February 2022. And as stated in the motion, Petitioners have also "collectively propounded 132 Special Interrogatories and 239 Document Requests" and Respondents have produced "over 200,000 documents, minimally redacted, and diligently organized." (Motion for Protective Order, 5:24-28.) The motion does not specify which portion of or demand from this propounded discovery warrants a protective order. The Court does not find a showing of good cause to issue a protective order barring all production of information and documents regarding Respondents' other clients. Respondents have not made a sufficient showing to warrant such a carte blanche protective order, and have not made a sufficient showing of good cause for a more tailored protective order. Further, through the parties' related meet and confer efforts, reasonable protections and conditions have already been applied to recent production demands related to Respondents' other client accounts - to wit, limiting the production period to 2019 and 2020, and allowing any specific client information, work product, or other privileged information to be redacted or omitted from production. Further, under Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.020(a), cited by Respondent, "The court shall limit the scope of discovery if it determines that the burden, expense, or intrusiveness of that discovery clearly outweighs the likelihood that the information sought will lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. The court may make this determination pursuant to a motion for protective order by a party or other affected person." Despite Respondent's objections and arguments against further production on the basis of undue burden, and alleged offers of alternative document production, the court does not find a sufficient showing to determine that the discovery sought related to Botto and Botto Law Groups' other client accounts is unduly burdensome or intrusive given the nature of the allegations in the Petition. [Part 2 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DELIA W. FOSTER 1995 TRUST TRUST

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DELIA W. FOSTER 1995 TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Respondent Botto Law Group To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One (Added For The Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] To be "discoverable," information must be "relevant to the subject matter" and reasonably calculated to lead to discovery of admissible evidence (CCP section 2017.010). Information is "relevant to the subject matter" if it might "reasonably assist a party in evaluating the case, preparing for trial, or facilitating settlement thereof." (City of Los Angeles v. Sup. Ct. (2017) 9 Cal.App.5th 272, 288.) Petitioners allege that Botto and Botto Law Group overbilled Foster and the Foster Trust intentionally and knowingly. The requested information might reasonably assist Petitioners in evaluating their allegations and - depending on what the information shows - may help facilitate settlement. While the information about other client account billing may or may not be admissible, it may lead to discovery of admissible evidence of the alleged breaches of duty and elder abuse. "[E]vidence of other transactions to show motive, intent, knowledge, plan, and absence of mistake" is admissible. (Kirkland v. Sup. Ct. (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 92, 98, quoting Morris Stulsaft Foundation v. Sup. Ct. (1966) 245 Cal.App.2d 409, 422-23.) As discussed in City of Los Angeles v. Sup. Ct. (2017) 9 Cal.App.5th 272, 288, the trial court may limit discovery that otherwise satisfies CCP section 2017.010 if it determines the discovery sought is "[(1)] unreasonably cumulative or duplicative, or is obtainable from some other source that is more convenient, less burdensome, or less expensive; or (2) The selected method of discovery is unduly burdensome or expensive, taking into account the needs of the case, the amount in controversy, and the importance of the issues at stake in the litigation." (Id., quoting CCP section 2019.030(a).) Although the Opposition argues that the discovery requested is unduly burdensome, there is insufficient showing for the court to determine how responding to the production request would be unduly burdensome. Respondent's objections, in addition to not being supported by any specific showing, are also undercut by both (1) Petitioners limiting the requested time period to 2019 and 2020, and (2) Respondent having offered a "summary chart" of paid invoices in lieu of the requested redacted original billing invoices. The original billing invoices presumably already exist, unlike a summary chart of all paid invoices which would ostensibly have to be created for the purposes of this discovery. Further, there is insufficient showing that a summary chart of all paid invoices such as Respondent has allegedly offered would be responsive to the actual document request, which is for daily attorney billing records to determine time billed per day, per attorney, in the years 2019 and 2020 - not merely the law firm's gross billing income in those years. Per the language of CCP section 2023.010 et seq, the court is required to impose a monetary sanction "against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully" opposes a motion to compel further response to a production demand, unless the court finds imposing the sanction would be unjust or the sanctioned party acted with substantial justification (CCP section 2031.310(i); see also 2031.320(b)). The court does not find grounds to support either of the exceptions to the mandatory imposition of monetary sanctions. Respondent Botto Law Group is ordered to pay $560 in sanctions to Petitioners within 10 days of service of Notice of Entry of this order. [Part 2 of 3]

(Id., quoting CCP section 2019.030(a).) rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For (1) Order Compelling Trustee To Provide Distributions From The Suport Trust; (2) Breach Of Trust And Removal Of Trustee And Appointment
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/14/22. (No earlier available date.) It appears that procedures for non-appearance ex parte petitions were not followed, as the ex parte petition filed on 5/20/22 has not been logged in on the tracking spreadsheet by the clerk and, therefore, has not been assigned to any examiner and adjudicated. [LRSF 14.11 B.] No further papers should be filed in this case as this petition and any subsequent filings should have been filed in the already existing case number, PTR-10-294131. After the ex parte petition to consolidate this case with the already existing case is granted, following local rules for non-appearance ex parte petitions, the matter can be heard. Examiners will not respond to any ex parte communication in a contested matter. [LRSF 7.10(b).] No "supplement" appears to have been filed either, either prior to the hearing on 12/20/21 or since that hearing, which supplement ostensibly addressed issues regarding certain beneficiaries. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KANTABEN IL PATEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/22/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please explain the relationship of Manilal Patel to Decedent. (Same issue from 04/27/2022 still hasn't been addressed) 2) Please explain the relationship of Jay Patel to Decedent. Jay Patel is listed as a co-tenant and son of Petitioner on notice but is not listed on the petition page 2 under #9. (Same issue from 04/27/2022 still hasn't been addressed) 3) Please explain the legal basis for the court transferring trust assets by a spousal property petition. 4) Please file a copy of the Ishverbhai N. Patel Living Trust dated 12/15/1999 and any and all amendments. 5) Please rebut the presumption in evidence code 662 for the real property held in a trust. The owner of the legal title to property is presumed to be the owner of the full beneficial title. This presumption may be rebutted only by clear and convincing proof. (Evidence Code 662). 6) Please rebut the presumption in evidence code 662 for the real property held in the LLC. The owner of the legal title to property is presumed to be the owner of the full beneficial title. This presumption may be rebutted only by clear and convincing proof. (Evidence Code 662). 7) Please rebut the presumption in evidence code 662 for all 3 bank accounts. The owner of the legal title to property is presumed to be the owner of the full beneficial title. This presumption may be rebutted only by clear and convincing proof. (Evidence Code 662). 8) Please explain the legal basis for the court determining the Bank of America account ending in #6701 is community property when there are three names listed on the account Ishverbhai N Patel, Jayeshkumar I Patel, and Kantaven I Patel. 9) Please file a June 2022 or July 2022 account statement for Bank of America account ending in #6701. 10) Please explain the legal basis for the court determining the Freemont Bank account ending in #9260 is community property when the only names on the account are Jayeshkumar Patel and Ishverbhai N. Patel. 11) Please file a June 2022 or July 2022 account statement for Freemont Bank account ending in #9260. 12) Please explain the legal basis for the court determining the Citibank account ending in #5083 is community property when the only names on the account are I N Patel or J I Patel. 13) Please file a June 2022 or July 2022 account statement for Citibank account ending in #5083. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/8/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LEONISA TRIAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/29/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Petitioner must give 15 days' notice. 2) Petitioner hasn't filed proof of publication. 3) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 4) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 5) On petition page 2 #5.a, petitioner must select either (3) registered domestic partner or (4) no registered domestic partner. 6) On petition page 2 #5.a , petitioner must select either (7) issue or a predeceased child or (8) no issue of a predeceased child. 7) The attachment 8 to the petition does not state the age of the persons listed. Please provide the age of all parties on attachment 8. 8) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed with a cover page, separately, and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/15/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
